devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । निर्वेदवादिनीमेवं मनोर्दुहितरं मुनिः । दयालुः शालिनीमाह शुक्लाभिव्याहृतं स्मरन् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācanirvedavādinīmevaṃ manorduhitaraṃ muniḥdayāluḥ śālinīmāha śuklābhivyāhṛtaṃ smaran
translation:
Maitreya said:
When the praiseworthy daughter of Manu spoke despondently, the merciful sage, remembering the words of Viṣṇu (of white complexion) addressed her thus:

devanagari: ऋषिरुवाच । मा खिदो राजपुत्रीत्थं आत्मानं प्रत्यनिन्दिते । भगवान् तेऽक्षरो गर्भं अदूरात्सम्प्रपत्स्यते ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣiruvācamā khido rājaputrītthaṃ ātmānaṃ pratyaninditebhagavān te'kṣaro garbhaṃ adūrātsamprapatsyate
translation:
The sage said:
Oh blameless princess, do not thus torment yourself (and me as well). The imperishable and immutable[1] Lord will soon enter your womb.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
akṣara—(i) Immutable. Hence unrelated to the chromosomes of the parents—Padaratnāvalī
(ii) Knowledge incarnate; the Inner Controller—antaryāmin—Subodhinī
(iii) Not assuming ordinary boḍy (due to its independence of the semen etc. of the parents)—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa

devanagari: धृतव्रतासि भद्रं ते दमेन नियमेन च । तपोद्रविणदानैश्च श्रद्धया चेश्वरं भज ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhṛtavratāsi bhadraṃ te damena niyamena catapodraviṇadānaiśca śraddhayā ceśvaraṃ bhaja
translation: You have been observing vows. God bless you. Be devoted to the Lord with faith, self-control (dama), niyama (observance of the ten prescribed restraints), penance and charitable donation of wealth.

devanagari: स त्वयाऽराधितः शुक्लो वितन्वन् मामकं यशः । छेत्ता ते हृदयग्रन्थिं औदर्यो ब्रह्मभावनः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvayā'rādhitaḥ śuklo vitanvan māmakaṃ yaśaḥchettā te hṛdayagranthiṃ audaryo brahmabhāvanaḥ
translation: Viṣṇu, so propitiated by you will be born to you as a son and spread my fame. He will instruct you about Brahman[2] and thus He will cut the knot (of ahaṃkāra) in your heart.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Brahma-bhāvana [bhāvanaḥ]—The creator of God Brahmā—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । देवहूत्यपि सन्देशं गौरवेण प्रजापतेः । सम्यक् श्रद्धाय पुरुषं कूटस्थं अभजद्‍गुरुम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācadevahūtyapi sandeśaṃ gauraveṇa prajāpateḥsamyak śraddhāya puruṣaṃ kūṭasthaṃ abhajad‍gurum
translation:
Maitreya said:
With great respect Devahūti trusted the advice of the Lord of Prajās (Kardama). She devoted herself to the immovable, unchangeable Supreme Soul, the preceptor[3] (of the Vedas).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
puru—(i) One who dispels ignorance (Bhāgavata Candrikā,Siddhāntapradīpa).
(ii) Propounder of knowledge about Brahman (Padaratnāvalī)

devanagari: तस्यां बहुतिथे काले भगवान् मधुसूदनः । कार्दमं वीर्यमापन्नो जज्ञेऽग्निरिव दारुणि ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ bahutithe kāle bhagavān madhusūdanaḥkārdamaṃ vīryamāpanno jajñe'gniriva dāruṇi
translation: After a lapse of long period, Lord Viṣṇu (Madhusūdana) was born of her by Kardama’s seed just as the fire does in a piece of wood.

devanagari: अवादयन् तदा व्योम्नि वादित्राणि घनाघनाः । गायन्ति तं स्म गन्धर्वा नृत्यन्ति अप्सरसो मुदा ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: avādayan tadā vyomni vāditrāṇi ghanāghanāḥgāyanti taṃ sma gandharvā nṛtyanti apsaraso mudā
translation: At that time, showering clouds appeared. Gods sounded the heavenly musical instruments in the sky. The celestial singers (Gandharvas) began to sing and the heavenly damsels (apsarās) danced in joy.

devanagari: पेतुः सुमनसो दिव्याः खेचरैः अपवर्जिताः । प्रसेदुश्च दिशः सर्वा अम्भांसि च मनांसि च ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: petuḥ sumanaso divyāḥ khecaraiḥ apavarjitāḥpraseduśca diśaḥ sarvā ambhāṃsi ca manāṃsi ca
translation: Heavenly flowers showered by gods (who move through the sky fell (on the earth). All directions became clear, waters translucent and minds tranquil.

devanagari: तत्कर्दमाश्रमपदं सरस्वत्या परिश्रितम् । स्वयम्भूः साकं ऋषिभिः मरीच्यादिभिरभ्ययात् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatkardamāśramapadaṃ sarasvatyā pariśritamsvayambhūḥ sākaṃ ṛṣibhiḥ marīcyādibhirabhyayāt
translation: God Brahmā accompanied by Marīci and other sages came to the hermitage of Kardama surrounded by the Sarasvatī.

devanagari: भगवन्तं परं ब्रह्म सत्त्वेनांशेन शत्रुहन् । तत्त्वसङ्ख्यानविज्ञप्त्यै जातं विद्वानजः स्वराट् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavantaṃ paraṃ brahma sattvenāṃśena śatruhantattvasaṅkhyānavijñaptyai jātaṃ vidvānajaḥ svarāṭ
translation: God Brahmā who has inborn perfect knowledge knew that the Supreme Lord had taken birth through his Sattva attribute for the promulgation of the true knowledge of the Principles (Sāṃkhya Śāstra), Oh Vidura (destroyer of enemies)!

devanagari: सभाजयन् विशुद्धेन चेतसा तच्चिकीर्षितम् । प्रहृष्यमाणैरसुभिः कर्दमं चेदमभ्यधात् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sabhājayan viśuddhena cetasā taccikīrṣitamprahṛṣyamāṇairasubhiḥ kardamaṃ cedamabhyadhāt
translation: With a pure heart, Brahmā expressed his respects for what he intended to do (viz. propagation of Sāṃkhya Śāstra). With his spirits (senses) ebullient with joy, he spoke to Kardama (and Devahūti) as follows:

devanagari: ब्रह्मोवाच । त्वया मेऽपचितिस्तात कल्पिता निर्व्यलीकतः । यन्मे सञ्जगृहे वाक्यं भवान्मानद मानयन् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmovācatvayā me'pacitistāta kalpitā nirvyalīkataḥyanme sañjagṛhe vākyaṃ bhavānmānada mānayan
translation:
Brahmā said:
Oh child, you have sincerely offered me worship in obeying respectfully my words. (In this way) you honour others.

devanagari: एतावत्येव शुश्रूषा कार्या पितरि पुत्रकैः । बाढं इति अनुमन्येत गौरवेण गुरोर्वचः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvatyeva śuśrūṣā kāryā pitari putrakaiḥbāḍhaṃ iti anumanyeta gauraveṇa gurorvacaḥ
translation: This much service should be rendered by children to their father, viz. the words of the father should respectfully and willingly be complied with.

devanagari: इमा दुहितरः सभ्य तव वत्स सुमध्यमाः । सर्गमेतं प्रभावैः स्वैः बृंहयिष्यन्ति अनेकधा ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: imā duhitaraḥ sabhya tava vatsa sumadhyamāḥsargametaṃ prabhāvaiḥ svaiḥ bṛṃhayiṣyanti anekadhā
translation: Oh courteous child, these beautiful daughters of yours will multiply manifold and increase the creation of the world with their progeny.

devanagari: अतस्त्वं ऋषिमुख्येभ्यो यथाशीलं यथारुचि । आत्मजाः परिदेह्यद्य विस्तृणीहि यशो भुवि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: atastvaṃ ṛṣimukhyebhyo yathāśīlaṃ yathāruciātmajāḥ paridehyadya vistṛṇīhi yaśo bhuvi
translation: Today, therefore, you give your daughters to the chief sages with proper regard to their tastes and character.

devanagari: वेदाहमाद्यं पुरुषं अवतीर्णं स्वमायया । भूतानां शेवधिं देहं बिभ्राणं कपिलं मुने ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vedāhamādyaṃ puruṣaṃ avatīrṇaṃ svamāyayābhūtānāṃ śevadhiṃ dehaṃ bibhrāṇaṃ kapilaṃ mune
translation: Oh sage, I know that the First Puruṣa (i.e. Viṣṇu) has incarnated through his Māyā and assumed the person (body) called Kapila who is a treasure (of blessings) to all beings.

devanagari: ज्ञानविज्ञानयोगेन कर्मणां उद्धरन्जटाः । हिरण्यकेशः पद्माक्षः पद्ममुद्रापदाम्बुजः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānavijñānayogena karmaṇāṃ uddharanjaṭāḥhiraṇyakeśaḥ padmākṣaḥ padmamudrāpadāmbujaḥ
translation: Oh daughter of Manu, this (Puruṣa) of golden locks of hair, of lotuslike eyes and of lotuslike feet marked with lotuses has entered your womb, for uprooting all karmas by means of the śāstric knowledge, Direct Perception or knowledge (vijñāna) and yoga. This enemy (killer) of Kaiṭabha will cut the knot of Nescience and doubt, and wander over the earth.

devanagari: एष मानवि ते गर्भं प्रविष्टः कैटभार्दनः । अविद्यासंशयग्रन्थिं छित्त्वा गां विचरिष्यति ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa mānavi te garbhaṃ praviṣṭaḥ kaiṭabhārdanaḥavidyāsaṃśayagranthiṃ chittvā gāṃ vicariṣyati
translation: Oh daughter of Manu, this (Puruṣa) of golden locks of hair, of lotuslike eyes and of lotuslike feet marked with lotuses has entered your womb, for uprooting all karmas by means of the śāstric knowledge, Direct Perception or knowledge (vijñāna) and yoga. This enemy (killer) of Kaiṭabha will cut the knot of Nescience and doubt, and wander over the earth.

devanagari: अयं सिद्धगणाधीशः साङ्ख्याचार्यैः सुसम्मतः । लोके कपिल इत्याख्यां गन्ता ते कीर्तिवर्धनः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ siddhagaṇādhīśaḥ sāṅkhyācāryaiḥ susammataḥloke kapila ityākhyāṃ gantā te kīrtivardhanaḥ
translation: He is the chief of the category of Siddhas (who have obtained Perfect Knowledge). He will be deeply respected by the Masters of the Sāṃkhya Śāstra.[4] He will be known as Kapila in the world. He will increase your fame.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
On the strength of Padma P. Padaratnāvalī, Kramasandarbha., VC., Dīpinī and Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa record that Kapila the founder of the atheistic Sāṃkhya is different from this teacher of the Theistic Sāṃkhya.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । तौ आवाश्वास्य जगत्स्रष्टा कुमारैः सहनारदः । हंसो हंसेन यानेन त्रिधामपरमं ययौ ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācatau āvāśvāsya jagatsraṣṭā kumāraiḥ sahanāradaḥhaṃso haṃsena yānena tridhāmaparamaṃ yayau
translation:
Maitreya said:
Cheering up the couple (Kardama-Devahūti) Brahmā, the creator of the world, with four Kumāras (Sanaka etc.) and Nārada mounted his swan-vehicle and went back to Satya- Ioka, the uppermost region above Svarga, the third world.

devanagari: गते शतधृतौ क्षत्तः कर्दमस्तेन चोदितः । यथोदितं स्वदुहितॄः प्रादाद्विश्वसृजां ततः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: gate śatadhṛtau kṣattaḥ kardamastena coditaḥyathoditaṃ svaduhitṝḥ prādādviśvasṛjāṃ tataḥ
translation: Oh Vidura, when god Brahmā departed, Kardama as per Brahmā’s instruction gave, as per Vedic rituals, his daughters to the procreators of the universe.

devanagari: मरीचये कलां प्रादाद् अनसूयां अथात्रये । श्रद्धां अङ्गिरसेऽयच्छत् पुलस्त्याय हविर्भुवम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: marīcaye kalāṃ prādād anasūyāṃ athātrayeśraddhāṃ aṅgirase'yacchat pulastyāya havirbhuvam
translation: He gave (in marriage) Kalā to Marīci, Anasūyā to Atri, Śraddhā to Aṅgirasas and married Havirbhū to Pulastya.

devanagari: पुलहाय गतिं युक्तां क्रतवे च क्रियां सतीम् । ख्यातिं च भृगवेऽयच्छद् वसिष्ठायाप्यरुन्धतीम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: pulahāya gatiṃ yuktāṃ kratave ca kriyāṃ satīmkhyātiṃ ca bhṛgave'yacchad vasiṣṭhāyāpyarundhatīm
translation: He gave Yuktā to Pulaha, the virtuous Kriyā to Kratu, Khyāti to Bhṛgu and Arundhatī to Vasiṣṭha.

devanagari: अथर्वणेऽददाच्छान्तिं यया यज्ञो वितन्यते । विप्रर्षभान् कृतोद्वाहान् सदारान् समलालयत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: atharvaṇe'dadācchāntiṃ yayā yajño vitanyateviprarṣabhān kṛtodvāhān sadārān samalālayat
translation: To Atharvan he gave Śānti who is the presiding deity of a Yajña (lit. who extends Yajña in the world). He satisfied the prominent Brāhmaṇas who were thus married and their wives as well.

devanagari: ततस्त ऋषयः क्षत्तः कृतदारा निमन्त्र्य तम् । प्रातिष्ठन् नन्दिमापन्नाः स्वं स्वमाश्रम मण्डलम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tatasta ṛṣayaḥ kṣattaḥ kṛtadārā nimantrya tamprātiṣṭhan nandimāpannāḥ svaṃ svamāśrama maṇḍalam
translation: Oh Vidura, then the sages, after the completion of marriages, took their leave of him (Kardama). They set out with great joy to their respective hermitages.

devanagari: स चावतीर्णं त्रियुगं आज्ञाय विबुधर्षभम् । विविक्त उपसङ्गम्य प्रणम्य समभाषत ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa cāvatīrṇaṃ triyugaṃ ājñāya vibudharṣabhamvivikta upasaṅgamya praṇamya samabhāṣata
translation: Having learnt that Viṣṇu, the great God, had taken incarnation, Kardama approached him while alone (privately), bowed to him and spoke:

devanagari: अहो पापच्यमानानां निरये स्वैरमङ्गलैः । कालेन भूयसा नूनं प्रसीदन्तीह देवताः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: aho pāpacyamānānāṃ niraye svairamaṅgalaiḥkālena bhūyasā nūnaṃ prasīdantīha devatāḥ
translation: “Oh! It is really after a very long time that gods are kindly disposed here to souls who are being roasted in the hell (-like Saṃsāra) by their own sins.

devanagari: बहुजन्मविपक्वेन सम्यग् योगसमाधिना । द्रष्टुं यतन्ते यतयः शून्यागारेषु यत्पदम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: bahujanmavipakvena samyag yogasamādhinādraṣṭuṃ yatante yatayaḥ śūnyāgāreṣu yatpadam
translation: Ascetics (who have renounced the world) try to visualize the Lord’s feet, in secluded places, by following correct course of concentration in the Bhakti-yoga (path of devotion) which has been properly developed by them through many (previous) births.

devanagari: स एव भगवानद्य हेलनं न गणय्य नः । गृहेषु जातो ग्राम्याणां यः स्वानां पक्षपोषणः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva bhagavānadya helanaṃ na gaṇayya naḥgṛheṣu jāto grāmyāṇāṃ yaḥ svānāṃ pakṣapoṣaṇaḥ
translation: That Supreme Lord who supports the cause of his devotees, is born today in the house of lowly (rustic) persons (like us), disregarding the humiliation therein.

devanagari: स्वीयं वाक्यमृतं कर्तुमं अतीर्णोऽसि मे गृहे । चिकीर्षुर्भगवान् ज्ञानं भक्तानां मानवर्धनः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: svīyaṃ vākyamṛtaṃ kartumaṃ atīrṇo'si me gṛhecikīrṣurbhagavān jñānaṃ bhaktānāṃ mānavardhanaḥ
translation: You have taken incarnation in my house for the purpose of proving true to your word, as well as for propagating the knowledge of the Sāṃkhya doctrine. (Thus) you increase the veneration for your devotees.

devanagari: तान्येव तेऽभिरूपाणि रूपाणि भगवंस्तव । यानि यानि च रोचन्ते स्वजनानां अरूपिणः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tānyeva te'bhirūpāṇi rūpāṇi bhagavaṃstavayāni yāni ca rocante svajanānāṃ arūpiṇaḥ
translation: [5] Oh Lord, although you are devoid of forms[6], whatever forms (e.g. four-armed god, etc.) are liked by your devotees, are the proper forms for you.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Oh Lord, you are devoid of ordinary or vulgar forms. Your superhuman forms (e.g. that of four-armed god etc.) are proper for you. (But) whatever form (e.g. that of a human being) is liked by your devotees is (also) liked by you.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

Footnote [6]:
arūpiṇaḥ [arūpin]—(i) Devoid of vulgar forms—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(ii) Having no form of god or man (etc.) which one gets due to karmas—Bhāgavata Candrikā Bālaprabodhini
(iii) The forms liked by your devotees are not your real forms. All your forms are due to Māyā. This is what Bhāvāratha Dīpikā means by Prākṛtarūpa- rahita [rahitaḥ],—VC.

devanagari: त्वां सूरिभिस्तत्त्वबुभुत्सयाद्धा । सदाभिवादार्हणपादपीठम् । ऐश्वर्यवैराग्ययशोऽवबोध । वीर्यश्रिया पूर्तमहं प्रपद्ये ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: tvāṃ sūribhistattvabubhutsayāddhāsadābhivādārhaṇapādapīṭhamaiśvaryavairāgyayaśo'vabodhavīryaśriyā pūrtamahaṃ prapadye
translation: I take shelter in you whose foot-stool is always worthy of paying obeisance by the learned ones who wish to perceive the Truth directly (as) you are perfect in the divine faculties (of omnipotence, omnipresence etc.), non-attachment, glory, knowledge, prowess (or strength) and majesty (or splendour).

devanagari: परं प्रधानं पुरुषं महान्तं । कालं कविं त्रिवृतं लोकपालम् । आत्मानुभूत्यानुगतप्रपञ्चं । स्वच्छन्दशक्तिं कपिलं प्रपद्ये ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: paraṃ pradhānaṃ puruṣaṃ mahāntaṃkālaṃ kaviṃ trivṛtaṃ lokapālamātmānubhūtyānugataprapañcaṃsvacchandaśaktiṃ kapilaṃ prapadye
translation: I resort to Kapila[7] who is the Supreme God. All the powers are within his control. He is in the form of Prakṛti, Puruṣa (who presides over Prakṛti), Mahat, Kala (Time—the agitator), Ahaṃkāra[8] (of three types as per three guṇas), the protector of the world. By his power of intelligence, he has absorbed the whole of the universe within himself. He is omniscient (mūla Brahma: a witness to the evolution and devolution of al] principles like Prakṛti etc.).
purport:
Footnote [7]:
One who protects ka (Brahmā) and pi (Pinakin—Śiva). Hence Viṣṇu—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [8]:
tri-vṛt—(i) One who is covered (described) by three Vedas—Padaratnāvalī
(ii) Of the form of jīva in whom development of three guṇas takes place—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: आ स्माभिपृच्छेऽद्य पतिं प्रजानां । त्वयावतीर्णर्ण उताप्तकामः । परिव्रजत्पदवीमास्थितोऽहं । चरिष्ये त्वां हृदि युञ्जन् विशोकः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: ā smābhipṛcche'dya patiṃ prajānāṃtvayāvatīrṇarṇa utāptakāmaḥparivrajatpadavīmāsthito'haṃcariṣye tvāṃ hṛdi yuñjan viśokaḥ
translation: You are the Lord of all creation. Hence I would like to ask something of you today. By you (being born as a son to me) I have repaid all the three debts (pertaining to gods, sages and forefathers). All of my desires have been really satisfied. Having taken to the path of Saṃnyāsa (recluse) I shall now wander meditating on you in my heart, free from all sorrow.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । मया प्रोक्तं हि लोकस्य प्रमाणं सत्यलौकिके । अथाजनि मया तुभ्यं यदवोचमृतं मुने ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācamayā proktaṃ hi lokasya pramāṇaṃ satyalaukikeathājani mayā tubhyaṃ yadavocamṛtaṃ mune
translation:
The Lord said:
Oh Sage, whatever I state about Vedic and worldly karmas[9] is authoritative to the world. It is for validating what I told (promised) to you that I am born (as your son).
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Satya-laukika: (i) Path leading to dharma, artha and kāma and mokṣa—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa
(ii) Brahman and the means of realizing it—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(iii) Means leading to Liberation (mukti) and enjoyment (bhukti)—Siddhāntapradīpa

devanagari: एतन्मे जन्म लोकेऽस्मिन् मुमुक्षूणां दुराशयात् । प्रसङ्ख्यानाय तत्त्वानां सम्मतायात्मदर्शने ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: etanme janma loke'smin mumukṣūṇāṃ durāśayātprasaṅkhyānāya tattvānāṃ sammatāyātmadarśane
translation: In this world, this incarnation of mine is for the exposition of the true knowledge of principles (like Prakṛti, Puruṣa etc.) leading to self-realization to those who seek liberation from the Liṅga śarīra (subtle body).

devanagari: एष आत्मपथोऽव्यक्तो नष्टः कालेन भूयसा । तं प्रवर्तयितुं देहं इमं विद्धि मया भृतम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa ātmapatho'vyakto naṣṭaḥ kālena bhūyasātaṃ pravartayituṃ dehaṃ imaṃ viddhi mayā bhṛtam
translation: This subtle course of realization of the Soul (ātman) is lost for a long period. Please note that it is for the reinstitution of that path that I have assumed this body.

devanagari: गच्छ कामं मयाऽपृष्टो मयि सन्न्यस्तकर्मणा । जित्वा सुदुर्जयं मृत्युं अमृतत्वाय मां भज ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: gaccha kāmaṃ mayā'pṛṣṭo mayi sannyastakarmaṇājitvā sudurjayaṃ mṛtyuṃ amṛtatvāya māṃ bhaja
translation: You have my permission. You may go at will. Dedicate all karmas tome. Having thereby conquered Death (i.e. saṃsāra) which is difficult to achieve, you resort to me for Liberation (Mokṣa);

devanagari: मामात्मानं स्वयंज्योतिः सर्वभूतगुहाशयम् । आत्मन्येवात्मना वीक्ष्य विशोकोऽभयमृच्छसि ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: māmātmānaṃ svayaṃjyotiḥ sarvabhūtaguhāśayamātmanyevātmanā vīkṣya viśoko'bhayamṛcchasi
translation: By your mind realize within yourself Me, the Supreme Soul, the self-illuminating Light within the (cave of) hearts of all beings. (Thereby) being free from sorrow you will attain to Liberation.

devanagari: मात्र आध्यात्मिकीं विद्यां शमनीं सर्वकर्मणाम् । वितरिष्ये यया चासौ भयं चातितरिष्यति ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: mātra ādhyātmikīṃ vidyāṃ śamanīṃ sarvakarmaṇāmvitariṣye yayā cāsau bhayaṃ cātitariṣyati
translation: I shall enunciate to my mother this spiritual lore which annihilates all karmas. She will thereby transgress Saṃsāra (cause of fear) and attain to the Highest bliss.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । एवं समुदितस्तेन कपिलेन प्रजापतिः । दक्षिणीकृत्य तं प्रीतो वनमेव जगाम ह ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaevaṃ samuditastena kapilena prajāpatiḥdakṣiṇīkṛtya taṃ prīto vanameva jagāma ha
translation:
Maitreya said:
Prajāpati Kardama who was thus addressed by Kapila, was pleased. He circumambulated Kapila and went to the forest.

devanagari: व्रतं स आस्थितो मौनं आत्मैकशरणो मुनिः । निःसङ्गो व्यचरत् क्षोणीं अनग्निरनिकेतनः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: vrataṃ sa āsthito maunaṃ ātmaikaśaraṇo muniḥniḥsaṅgo vyacarat kṣoṇīṃ anagniraniketanaḥ
translation: The Sage took the vow (e.g. ahiṃsā etc.) of the recluse’s way of life. He took resort only in the Supreme Soul. Free from attachment and renouncing fire-worship and home, he wandered all over the world.

devanagari: मनो ब्रह्मणि युञ्जानो यत्तत् सदसतः परम् । गुणावभासे विगुण एकभक्त्यानुभाविते ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: mano brahmaṇi yuñjāno yattat sadasataḥ paramguṇāvabhāse viguṇa ekabhaktyānubhāvite
translation: He concentrated his mind on the Brahman which is different from cause and effect, which (though) devoid of attributes—(guṇas)—manifests the three guṇas and which is experienced only by complete unswerving devotion. Kardama became free from ahaṃkāra (ego), attachment. He transcended the pairs of opposites (e.g. pleasure-pain; heat-cold). He looked upon all equally and concentrated himself on self-realization only. His mental capacities turned inward (to Brahman) and became calm (and serene). Full of wisdom, he was (quiet) like a calm ocean unperturbed by waves. With highest devotion to Lord Vāsudeva, the Omniscient, the inner controller (antaryāmin) of all beings, he realized his Supreme Soul and became free from bondage (of ignorance).

devanagari: निरहङ्कृतिर्निर्ममश्च निर्द्वन्द्वः समदृक् स्वदृक् । प्रत्यक्प्रशान्तधीर्धीरः प्रशान्तोर्मिरिवोदधिः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: nirahaṅkṛtirnirmamaśca nirdvandvaḥ samadṛk svadṛkpratyakpraśāntadhīrdhīraḥ praśāntormirivodadhiḥ
translation: He concentrated his mind on the Brahman which is different from cause and effect, which (though) devoid of attributes—(guṇas)—manifests the three guṇas and which is experienced only by complete unswerving devotion. Kardama became free from ahaṃkāra (ego), attachment. He transcended the pairs of opposites (e.g. pleasure-pain; heat-cold). He looked upon all equally and concentrated himself on self-realization only. His mental capacities turned inward (to Brahman) and became calm (and serene). Full of wisdom, he was (quiet) like a calm ocean unperturbed by waves. With highest devotion to Lord Vāsudeva, the Omniscient, the inner controller (antaryāmin) of all beings, he realized his Supreme Soul and became free from bondage (of ignorance).

devanagari: वासुदेवे भगवति सर्वज्ञे प्रत्यगात्मनि । परेण भक्तिभावेन लब्धात्मा मुक्तबन्धनः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudeve bhagavati sarvajñe pratyagātmanipareṇa bhaktibhāvena labdhātmā muktabandhanaḥ
translation: He concentrated his mind on the Brahman which is different from cause and effect, which (though) devoid of attributes—(guṇas)—manifests the three guṇas and which is experienced only by complete unswerving devotion. Kardama became free from ahaṃkāra (ego), attachment. He transcended the pairs of opposites (e.g. pleasure-pain; heat-cold). He looked upon all equally and concentrated himself on self-realization only. His mental capacities turned inward (to Brahman) and became calm (and serene). Full of wisdom, he was (quiet) like a calm ocean unperturbed by waves. With highest devotion to Lord Vāsudeva, the Omniscient, the inner controller (antaryāmin) of all beings, he realized his Supreme Soul and became free from bondage (of ignorance).

devanagari: आत्मानं सर्वभूतेषु भगवन्तं अवस्थितम् । अपश्यत्सर्वभूतानि भगवत्यपि चात्मनि ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānaṃ sarvabhūteṣu bhagavantaṃ avasthitamapaśyatsarvabhūtāni bhagavatyapi cātmani
translation: He saw his Supreme Soul abiding in all beings and realized all beings within God and within his own Self (Atman) as well.

devanagari: इच्छाद्वेषविहीनेन सर्वत्र समचेतसा । भगवद्‍भक्तियुक्तेन प्राप्ता भागवती गतिः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: icchādveṣavihīnena sarvatra samacetasābhagavad‍bhaktiyuktena prāptā bhāgavatī gatiḥ
translation:
He became free from desires and hatred (likes and dislikes). He looked upon all as equals. With highest devotion to the Lord, he attained Liberation (place where all bhāgavatas reach).
[back to top]
